tendons.name: Achilles Tendon
tendons.anatomical_description: The Achilles tendon is a tough band of fibrous tissue that connects the calf muscles to the heel bone (calcaneus).

tendons.name: Patellar Tendon
tendons.anatomical_description: The patellar tendon connects the bottom of the kneecap (patella) to the top of the shinbone (tibia).

tendons.name: Hamstring Tendons
tendons.anatomical_description: The hamstring tendons connect the hamstring muscles to the back of the knee and the pelvis.

tendons.name: Hip Flexor Tendons
tendons.anatomical_description: The hip flexor tendons connect the hip flexor muscles to the front of the hip and thigh.
tendons.common_issues: Hip flexor tendinopathy, hip flexor strains.

tendons.name: Rotator Cuff Tendons
tendons.anatomical_description: The rotator cuff is a group of four tendons and muscles that surround the shoulder joint, keeping the head of the upper arm bone firmly within the shallow socket of the shoulder.
tendons.common_issues: Rotator cuff tendinopathy, rotator cuff tears.

tendons.name: Biceps Tendon
tendons.anatomical_description: The biceps tendon connects the biceps muscle to the shoulder and elbow.

tendons.name: Tennis Elbow (Lateral Epicondyle)
tendons.anatomical_description: The lateral epicondyle is the bony bump on the outside of the elbow. The tendons that attach to this point are responsible for extending the wrist and fingers.

tendons.name: Golfer's Elbow (Medial Epicondyle)
tendons.anatomical_description: The medial epicondyle is the bony bump on the inside of the elbow. The tendons that attach to this point are responsible for flexing the wrist and fingers.

tendons.name: Hand Tendons
tendons.anatomical_description: The hand has a complex network of tendons that control the movement of the fingers and wrist.
tendons.common_issues: Tenosynovitis, trigger finger, De Quervain's tenosynovitis.

tendons.name: Foot Tendons
tendons.anatomical_description: The foot has numerous tendons that support the arch and control the movement of the toes and ankle.
tendons.common_issues: Plantar fasciitis, posterior tibial tendon dysfunction.

exercises.load_guidance: Start with two legs, progress to single leg. Push back against the wall and foot into the ground to generate force. Aim for ~70% of max effort.
exercises.progression_steps: 1. Two-legged wall sit. 2. Single-leg wall sit. 3. Increase hold duration. 4. Add weight (e.g., holding a dumbbell).
exercises.form_cues: Keep your back flat against the wall. Your knee should be directly above your ankle.
exercises.safety_notes: If single-leg is too difficult, start with two legs. Stop if you feel sharp pain.

exercises.joint_angle: Pushing against an immovable object at the bottom of the leg extension machine.
exercises.load_guidance: Push as hard as possible for the duration of the hold.
exercises.progression_steps: Increase the force of the contraction.
exercises.form_cues: Focus on contracting the quadriceps muscle.
exercises.safety_notes: Ensure the machine is set up correctly and the weight is appropriate.

exercises.sets: Not specified, but likely multiple sets.
exercises.joint_angle: Front leg at 90 degrees, back knee down. To target Achilles, move front knee forward.
exercises.load_guidance: Start with support (e.g., hands on a chair), progress to no support.
exercises.progression_steps: 1. Use two hands for support. 2. Use one hand for support. 3. No hands for support. 4. Increase hold duration.
exercises.form_cues: Keep your torso upright. Your front knee should be aligned with your front foot.
exercises.safety_notes: Use support as needed to maintain balance. Stop if you feel sharp pain.

exercises.sets: Not specified, but likely multiple sets.
exercises.joint_angle: Squat down to a comfortable depth.
exercises.load_guidance: Start with support (e.g., hands on a chair), progress to no support.
exercises.progression_steps: 1. Use support. 2. No support. 3. Increase hold duration. 4. Add weight.
exercises.form_cues: Keep your chest up and your back straight. Your feet should be shoulder-width apart.
exercises.safety_notes: Use support as needed. Don't go too deep if you feel pain.

exercises.sets: Not specified, but likely multiple sets.
exercises.joint_angle: Hold the push-up position at a desired height.
exercises.load_guidance: Start with an incline (e.g., against a wall or table), progress to the floor.
exercises.progression_steps: 1. Incline push-up hold. 2. Floor push-up hold. 3. Increase hold duration.
exercises.form_cues: Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels. Your hands should be shoulder-width apart.
exercises.safety_notes: Start with an incline to reduce the load. Stop if you feel sharp pain in your shoulders or elbows.

exercises.joint_angle: Pushing against a wall with toes cocked, feet flat, and knees bent or straight.
exercises.load_guidance: Focus on feeling tension without pain.
exercises.progression_steps: Increase the force of the contraction.
exercises.form_cues: Keep your heel on the ground. Lean into the wall to increase the stretch.
exercises.safety_notes: Stop if you feel sharp pain.

exercises.sets: Not specified, but likely multiple sets.
exercises.joint_angle: Hold the hamstring curl at a specific angle.
exercises.load_guidance: Use a resistance that allows you to hold the contraction for the desired duration.
exercises.form_cues: Focus on contracting the hamstring muscles.
exercises.safety_notes: Ensure the band or machine is set up correctly.

exercises.sets: Not specified, but likely multiple sets.
exercises.joint_angle: Hold the hip flexion at a specific angle.
exercises.load_guidance: Use a resistance that allows you to hold the contraction for the desired duration.
exercises.form_cues: Focus on contracting the hip flexor muscles.
exercises.safety_notes: Ensure the band or machine is set up correctly.

exercises.sets: Not specified, but likely multiple sets.
exercises.joint_angle: Hold the external rotation at a specific angle.
exercises.load_guidance: Use a resistance that allows you to hold the contraction for the desired duration.
exercises.form_cues: Keep your elbow at your side.
exercises.safety_notes: Don't use a weight that is too heavy.

exercises.sets: Not specified, but likely multiple sets.
exercises.joint_angle: Hold the biceps curl at a specific angle (e.g., 90 degrees).
exercises.load_guidance: Use a weight or resistance that allows you to hold the contraction for the desired duration.
exercises.form_cues: Keep your elbow at your side.
exercises.safety_notes: Don't use a weight that is too heavy.

exercises.sets: Not specified, but likely multiple sets.
exercises.joint_angle: Hold the wrist in an extended position.
exercises.load_guidance: Use a light weight or resistance.
exercises.form_cues: Keep your forearm supported on a table.
exercises.safety_notes: Stop if you feel sharp pain.

exercises.sets: Not specified, but likely multiple sets.
exercises.joint_angle: Hold the wrist in a flexed position.
exercises.load_guidance: Use a light weight or resistance.
exercises.form_cues: Keep your forearm supported on a table.
exercises.safety_notes: Stop if you feel sharp pain.

progress_indicators: Pain reduction (e.g., pain less than 3/10 on a numeric pain-rating scale).
Return to baseline pain levels 24 hours after loading.
For Achilles tendon repair, isokinetic/isometric strength greater than 90% Limb Symmetry Index (LSI).

rehab_to_maintenance: Ability to perform sport-specific movements without pain.
Demonstrated tolerance to full training load.
Completion of a return-to-run program without pain or swelling.
Lower extremity functional tests at or above 90% compared to the contralateral side for unilateral tests.
Psychological readiness to return to sport.
transition_criteria.maintenance_protocol_details: Maintenance protocols involve continuing with the exercises that were effective during rehab, but with a reduced frequency (e.g., 2-3 times per week). The focus is on maintaining strength and tendon health, and preventing re-injury. It is also important to continue with a balanced training program that includes both fast and slow movements to maintain tendon stiffness and compliance.
